title: alfaroa roxburghiana
intro: Alfaroa roxburghiana, also known as Roxburgh's white beech, is a plant species belonging to the family Juglandaceae. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Alfaroa roxburghiana include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include decoctions and infusions of its bark and leaves, which can be used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
